title: Events, Refs, SHAs, Forks, and Workflow Trust Boundaries · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Event Payload Ref Sha
summary: A maintainer uses pull_request_target to check out and execute fork code with secrets, then trusts a workflow_run artifact because its producer finished successfully. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci event payload ref sha increment in event and revision trust policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive workflow source, checked-out revision, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_SHA, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger.
Combine schema validation, action input review, expression linting, local deterministic inspection, changed-event fixtures, and GitHub-run evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. Competency probe: Derive workflow source, checked-out revision, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_SHA, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger. A defensible solution applies Ci Event Payload Ref Sha, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to event and revision trust policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-event-payload-ref-sha — Derive workflow source, checked-out revision, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_SHA, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-events-refs-trust-boundaries-3
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-events-refs-trust-boundaries-3
    artifact: event-trust-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha events refs trust boundaries 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      event: pull_request
      workflow-source: merge-commit
      checkout-revision: pull-request-merge-sha
      token: read-only
      secrets: withheld
      untrusted-code: isolated-execution
      privileged-followup: data-only
      producer-proof: run-id-and-workflow-sha
    verify: compare pull_request pull_request_target workflow_run refs SHAs tokens secrets and checked-out code
    failure: Reject fork code under pull_request_target secrets, maintainer rerun as trust, or successful artifacts as executable proof.
    repair: Use low-privilege pull_request execution, separate privileged follow-up, and validate producer and content strictly as data.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: push, pull_request, pull_request_target, and workflow_run use the same workflow file and SHA. This fails the Ci Event Payload Ref Sha contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
